bird.name: Thyolo alethe
bird.summary: The Thyolo alethe or Cholo alethe (Chamaetylas choloensis) is an endangered species of bird in the Old World flycatcher family Muscicapidae…

relations.subject: Thyolo alethe
relations.evidence: On Mount Namuli, the species was formerly common in mid-altitude forest at elevations of 1,200–1,400 m, but this habitat has now been almost entirely destroyed. The species primarily inhabits the ground layer of submontane and montane forests, especially moist submontane forests at elevations of 1,000–1,700 meters
relations.object_summary: 以成熟阔叶林和混交林为主的山地森林系统。

relations.subject: Thyolo alethe
relations.evidence: It was previously more widespread in both countries, but has had its range reduced due to the deforestation of many of the submontane forests it inhabits
relations.object_summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。
